title: Acute Appendicitis — RLQ Pain
personality: Polite but distracted by pain; answers briefly unless prompted; slightly anxious.
chief_complaint: Worsening stomach pain for most of a day.
history_of_present_illness: The abdominal pain began about 18 hours ago.
It started around the belly button and then moved to the lower right side.
The pain is constant now and worse with movement.
It hurts more when walking or going over bumps in the car.
associated_symptoms: One episode of vomiting this morning.
Low-grade fever at home (felt warm, did not measure).
Decreased appetite; skipped lunch and dinner yesterday.
negatives: No urinary burning, frequency, or flank pain.

physical_exam_findings.student_finding: The patient winces and reports sharp tenderness in the right lower quadrant.
There is mild diffuse abdominal tenderness with focal worsening toward the right lower quadrant.
Sudden release of deep pressure in the RLQ reproduces sharp pain (rebound tenderness).
You note mild voluntary guarding over the right lower quadrant.
Lung fields are clear to auscultation bilaterally with normal work of breathing.
The patient appears uncomfortable and diaphoretic; mucous membranes moist.

hidden_red_flags: Possible perforated viscus if pain suddenly improves with spreading rigidity (not yet present in this vignette).
Consider alternative diagnoses if urinary symptoms were present (not in this case).
emotional_profile: Anxious and uncomfortable; worried this could be serious but trying to stay cooperative.

checklist.text: Elicit pain migration from periumbilical region to RLQ
Clarify timing and progression of symptoms
Ask about nausea, vomiting, fever, appetite
Palpate RLQ and describe focal tenderness/guarding
Assess for rebound tenderness when clinically appropriate
Acknowledge patient discomfort and explain exam steps

patient_utterances_by_fact.pain_location: Right now the worst spot is my lower right side of my belly.
patient_utterances_by_fact.pain_migration: It actually started more around my belly button, then it kind of traveled down to the right lower area.
patient_utterances_by_fact.pain_onset: This started yesterday evening, so it’s been going on for maybe 18 hours and it’s been getting worse.
patient_utterances_by_fact.nausea: Yeah, I’ve felt nauseous since last night.
patient_utterances_by_fact.vomiting: I threw up once this morning.
patient_utterances_by_fact.fever: I felt warm at home. I don’t know the exact number.
patient_utterances_by_fact.appetite: I really haven’t wanted to eat much since yesterday.
patient_utterances_by_fact.neg_urinary: No burning with urination and I’m not going all the time.
patient_utterances_by_fact.default: I’m not sure how to answer that—my belly just hurts a lot, especially on the right lower side.
